title: Allow running exec with no attach streams requested
body:
This is a small Docker compat fix with the API exec endpoints. With Docker, sending a bare Exec Create (command only, nothing else set) and then an Exec Start without Detach set performs a detached exec. With Podman, we threw an error that at least one stream must be attached to. Fix is trivial; look up the session before start, check the config for attach streams, and force detach on if none are set.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
Fixed  a bug where the Exec Start endpoint would return an error when trying to start an exec session with no standard streams attached without specifying `Detach=true`
```

comments.body: Though, now that I think about this, I only checked `Detach=true` and not setting it at all. I should check `Detach=false` and see if Docker hard-errors in that case.

After investigation, Docker handles `Detach=false` being explicitly set the exact same as `Detach` not being set at all - if no streams were requested, no hijack takes place and we just return - standard detached exec.
The changes LGTM in general, but the tests are very red.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Further discovery: Docker allows at least some things (like Detach) to be passed as query parameters (despite being body parameters); we don't. Docker allows an empty request body; we don't. I will address these tomorrow.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
